seo.title: Mino Translator | Bilingual YouTube Subtitles for Chrome
seo.description: Mino Translator is a Chrome extension for bilingual YouTube subtitles and in-page selection translation. Bring your own AI model.
seo.ogDescription: Bilingual subtitles for YouTube videos and instant translation for selected text on any webpage.

hero.status: Not on the Chrome Web Store yet
hero.title: A smarter way to translate YouTube
hero.subtitle: Bilingual subtitles for videos, instant translation for any text you select, with a clear ZIP install guide.
hero.installTitle: From download to ready in 4 steps
hero.installText: Currently distributed as a ZIP package. Download, unzip, load the unpacked extension in Chrome, and add your API key.

features.title: There when you need it, invisible when you do not
features.body: Mino breaks subtitles, selection translation, and model setup into clean workflows that stay out of your way.

items.title: Bilingual YouTube subtitles
items.body: Overlay both source and translated subtitles on the player, synced to the current playback position.

items.title: Selection translation
items.body: Select any text on a page and a lightweight translation bubble appears. Read and move on.

items.title: Bring your own model
items.body: Compatible with any OpenAI-compatible API. Choose the provider you trust.

items.title: ZIP install
items.body: A clear manual install path before the store listing — no waiting for approval.

items.title: Customizable subtitle appearance
items.body: Subtitle mode, font size, position, background opacity, and color — all adjustable in settings.

scenes.youtubeTitle: Subtitles that work like a tool should
scenes.youtubeBody: Bilingual subtitles overlay the video with adjustable opacity and font size. You focus on the content — Mino handles the translation.
scenes.youtubeAlt: Bilingual subtitles on a YouTube video player
scenes.popupTitle: Popup for the actions that matter
scenes.popupBody: Open a captioned YouTube video and Mino detects available tracks automatically. Click the Mino icon to open the panel, choose your target language, and start translating. When translation completes, a lightweight notification appears in the corner — no tab switching, no interruption.
scenes.popupAlt: Extension popup showing an active translation task

download.title: Download and load into Chrome manually
download.body: Mino is not yet listed on the Chrome Web Store. Download the ZIP, unzip it, and load the unpacked extension in developer mode.

install.title: Install in four clear steps
install.body: Manual installation takes a few extra clicks — we've laid out every step so you can follow along.

steps.title: Download ZIP
steps.body: Get the latest package from the website.

steps.title: Unzip folder
steps.body: Extract it somewhere easy to find.

steps.title: Load unpacked
steps.body: Open chrome://extensions and enable developer mode.

steps.title: Add API key
steps.body: Enter API URL, API key, and model ID in settings.

api.title: Your model, your rules
api.body: Mino supports any OpenAI-compatible API. The website has no accounts and never stores your API key.
points: The website does not provide API keys
Configure your OpenAI-compatible API in extension settings
API keys stay in local browser storage only

items.question: Is Mino on the Chrome Web Store?
items.answer: Not yet. The website currently provides a ZIP package with a detailed manual install guide.

items.question: Why do I need developer mode?
items.answer: Because the extension is not listed on the Chrome Web Store. ZIP installation requires enabling developer mode and loading the unpacked folder.

items.question: Do I need my own API key?
items.answer: Yes. You need to configure an OpenAI-compatible API provider. The website does not provide model accounts or API keys.

items.question: Which languages are supported?
items.answer: Translation capabilities depend on your configured model. The website UI supports Simplified Chinese and English.

items.question: Which websites are supported?
items.answer: YouTube is the core scenario for subtitles. Selection translation works on most common webpages.

items.question: Can it translate videos without captions?
items.answer: No. Subtitle translation depends on the video having a caption track. Without one, subtitles cannot be generated.

items.question: Does the website receive my API key?
items.answer: No. The website has no login system and does not process your translation data. All requests go directly to your configured API provider.

items.question: How do I update the extension?
items.answer: Download the new ZIP, unzip it, and reload the unpacked extension folder in Chrome extension management.

items.title: No account needed
items.body: The website does not offer sign-up or login.

items.title: Text stays private
items.body: Translation text is never sent to the website.

items.title: Bring your own provider
items.body: Requests go directly to your configured API provider.

items.title: Local storage only
items.body: API keys are stored locally by the browser extension.

footer.note: Chrome extension website, currently distributed via ZIP package.
